- The Hunt for Lux: The Internet’s Most Disturbed User: Kind: captions Language: en-US It’s 2011, and Anonymous has just initiated&nbsp; “Operation Darknet”. They are launching attacks&nbsp;&nbsp; against a•use websites, publicly shaming them, and&nbsp; calling their users “disgusting degenerates". This&nbsp;&nbsp; move was highly praised, but what they didn’t&nbsp; realize is that it unintentionally worked like…&nbsp;&nbsp; an advertisement. Anonymous WAS criticizing&nbsp; these websites, but they had also made them&nbsp;&nbsp; famous in the process, and the news reached some&nbsp; of the wrong individuals. One of those people,&nbsp;&nbsp; known only by his username “Lux”, began to&nbsp; download the Tor browser, and he entered the&nbsp;&nbsp; dark web for the first time. He was intrigued by&nbsp; the horrific videos he found there, marking the&nbsp;&nbsp; start of an offender who became one of the most…&nbsp; depraved individuals to ever walk the earth. This&nbsp;&nbsp; is the story of the worldwide hunt for the king&nbsp; of hurtcore, and the twisted man behind the online&nbsp;&nbsp; persona… known as “Lux.” <> Chapter: Descent into Darkness&nbsp; “At first I felt ashamed in [sic] myself for&nbsp; being attracted to such a thing but as time&nbsp;&nbsp; went on I slowly grew more accepting&nbsp; of myself.” This is Lux describing…&nbsp;&nbsp; his attraction to miners. When he accessed the&nbsp; dark web he found endless videos of this type,&nbsp;&nbsp; and over time, it seems that he became&nbsp; desensitized to it. He quickly got to&nbsp;&nbsp; the point where he was quote “able to truly&nbsp; feel comfortable with [these] attractions.” After fully submerging himself in the dark web&nbsp; though, Lux began complaining. It seems that&nbsp;&nbsp; he was really bothered by two major problems&nbsp; he had noticed on these websites. Problem #1&nbsp;&nbsp; was that they lacked proper security, and&nbsp; as a computer expert himself, he knew they&nbsp;&nbsp; needed to do better. He was probably worried about&nbsp; getting caught as a user, but also realized that&nbsp;&nbsp; the website owners would also be VERY concerned&nbsp; about their anonymity. Therefore he gladly offered&nbsp;&nbsp; up his technical skills to improve their security&nbsp; measures, and this seemed to make his status rise&nbsp;&nbsp; very quickly, to the point where his skills became&nbsp; “in demand.” After all, security had just become&nbsp;&nbsp; especially important because Operation Darknet&nbsp; was drawing so much attention from the general&nbsp;&nbsp; public. Lux became addicted to this newfound level&nbsp; of attention, and as time went on, he got even&nbsp;&nbsp; more inquiries from website owners. He eventually&nbsp; worked on most of the top sites, and even educated&nbsp;&nbsp; other users on how to do basic things like&nbsp; stripping the metadata out of video uploads. After he gained a little notoriety, Lux&nbsp; decided it was time to tackle the 2nd&nbsp;&nbsp; problem on his list; and that was… he was upset&nbsp; that he couldn’t find videos involving… p•in.&nbsp;&nbsp; A few months after entering the dark web, he&nbsp; had probably watched hundreds of videos, but&nbsp;&nbsp; since these sites had banned people from posting&nbsp; h•rm, it might’ve felt... too vanilla for him.&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux wanted something EXTREME, and so in&nbsp; the coming months, he began developing&nbsp;&nbsp; his own websites that would host this type of&nbsp; content. He thought that this would bring him&nbsp;&nbsp; even more fame and power within the community,&nbsp; and chillingly, he would turn out to be correct. In 2012, Lux published MULTIPLE sites&nbsp; of his own creation onto the dark web.&nbsp;&nbsp; These sites pioneered his new favorite genre&nbsp; called “hurtcore”, which comes from combining the&nbsp;&nbsp; two words hurt and hardcore. In addition, all of&nbsp; his sites were exclusively.. C•. He quickly became&nbsp;&nbsp; known as the best source for such content, and&nbsp; hundreds of users flooded in to watch the videos.&nbsp;&nbsp; I can’t really add any more detail&nbsp; about the stuff that was being posted,&nbsp;&nbsp; but you can basically assume it was&nbsp; the worst possible type of criminal&nbsp;&nbsp; content imaginable. It was accurately&nbsp; described this way in a Vice headline. Chapter: The Hunt For Lux After Lux fully established&nbsp;&nbsp; his websites, the hurtcore videos began to draw&nbsp; significant attention from law enforcement. They&nbsp;&nbsp; felt like they needed to take action soon, as&nbsp; Operation Darknet and the Silk Road drama had&nbsp;&nbsp; substantially increased traffic on the dark web.&nbsp; By 2013, the FBI made a big move as they seized&nbsp;&nbsp; control and shut down the biggest C• server,&nbsp; known as Freedom Hosting. Lux was using this&nbsp;&nbsp; service to run his websites, and so in one quick&nbsp; action, all of his hurtcore was taken down. The&nbsp;&nbsp; FBI planted “custom malware designed to identify&nbsp; visitors” prior to shutting down the servers,&nbsp;&nbsp; so this became a turning point in the&nbsp; community as tons of people had their&nbsp;&nbsp; identities exposed and hundreds of websites were&nbsp; taken down. Unfortunately, Lux was not exposed by&nbsp;&nbsp; this malware though, and after a surprisingly&nbsp; short time, he was actually able to recover,&nbsp;&nbsp; and he re-established his websites. Lux invested&nbsp; in building his own personal hosting service,&nbsp;&nbsp; and he proceeded to migrate all of&nbsp; his sites and activities to bring&nbsp;&nbsp; them back online. He was extremely proud&nbsp; of this, and went on to taunt the FBI: “Well, it looks like this Empire hasn’t fallen&nbsp; yet! To any LEA whom [sic] may be reading this,&nbsp;&nbsp; F you. You can not keep us down, and every time&nbsp; you try we will just get bigger and bigger,&nbsp;&nbsp; so thanks for the publicity and leading more&nbsp; PDF files to where you can't catch them. To my&nbsp;&nbsp; fellow PDF files: it won’t be long now until all&nbsp; of the major C• sites are back up and running. Lux continued opening more and more sites to&nbsp; host hurtcore content in the following months. He&nbsp;&nbsp; actually made so many websites that he declared&nbsp; it was now an empire, referring to his portfolio&nbsp;&nbsp; as the “P3doEmpire”. This included his&nbsp; most infamous site named “hurt2thecore”,&nbsp;&nbsp; alongside other similar names that&nbsp; ALSO referenced the hurtcore genre.&nbsp;&nbsp; He was obsessed with building out this suite of&nbsp; websites and spent hours every night trying to&nbsp;&nbsp; optimize every detail, even though he feared&nbsp; this would inevitably have consequences… Chapter: The Hunt Intensifies The takedown of Freedom Hosting was a huge win&nbsp;&nbsp; for FBI and global law enforcement, but after Lux&nbsp; taunted them and created his own personal hosting&nbsp;&nbsp; service, the HUNT intensified. It was clear that&nbsp; the hurtcore content was even more extreme and&nbsp;&nbsp; brutal than the stuff on Freedom Hosting, so it&nbsp; became a high priority. Lux was added to the list&nbsp;&nbsp; of the FBI’s “most wanted” offenders. However,&nbsp; investigators quickly discovered that getting&nbsp;&nbsp; any information on Lux was incredibly difficult.&nbsp; Although he was seen active on the websites for&nbsp;&nbsp; hours everyday, there seemed to be conflicting&nbsp; information about his true identity. For example,&nbsp;&nbsp; the timestamps on his activity suggested that&nbsp; he might be located in the Southern Hemisphere,&nbsp;&nbsp; but when reviewing his messages, they saw that&nbsp; Lux claimed to be a Doctor in the United States.&nbsp;&nbsp; Many of his posts made references to how he was&nbsp; a 50-year old pediatrician, and he explained how&nbsp;&nbsp; his regular interaction with children would give&nbsp; him the perfect environment for e•ploitation. In&nbsp;&nbsp; multiple posts, he claimed to have a 6-year old&nbsp; girlfriend. With these statements aside though,&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux was very good at hiding his personal details,&nbsp; and he would never visually show himself on&nbsp;&nbsp; any of the websites. The FBI and other global&nbsp; agencies used a bunch of different identification&nbsp;&nbsp; strategies, but it felt like they were searching&nbsp; for a ghost. They kept trying though, as they&nbsp;&nbsp; were definitely motivated to catch the man they&nbsp; called “ one of the most prolific offenders ever… Chapter: The Empire Expands&nbsp; The Lux empire continued to grow as time went&nbsp; on, and the new additions demonstrated new&nbsp;&nbsp; levels of depravity. One site he built was&nbsp; a wikipedia specifically dedicated to MAPs,&nbsp;&nbsp; or miner-attracted persons. This wiki contained&nbsp; 600 articles on topics surrounding e•ploitation,&nbsp;&nbsp; and over one thousand users went&nbsp; on to make contributions to the&nbsp;&nbsp; site. Their contributions defined and&nbsp; provided examples of hurtcore content,&nbsp;&nbsp; and also… guides on how to create your own. There&nbsp; was even a section on how to dispose of… r•mains.&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux probably saw this as like a complimentary&nbsp; handbook to help explain the things on the other&nbsp;&nbsp; websites, and by framing it as an educational&nbsp; resource, the wiki seemed to normalize everything&nbsp;&nbsp; in the empire. It became popular, to the point&nbsp; where the wiki was visited 3 million times. Chapter: Hurt2TheCore&nbsp; Many of the empire websites were considered evil,&nbsp; but none of them were as disturbing, or popular,&nbsp;&nbsp; as hurt2thecore. The site hosted videos&nbsp; and had forums where users would interact,&nbsp;&nbsp; but it was really the practices and rules that&nbsp; really made it stand out. All new users would&nbsp;&nbsp; be restricted to only the surface-level,&nbsp; with a very limited amount of content.&nbsp;&nbsp; In order to gain access to the so-called “premium”&nbsp; areas, they would need to create and upload their&nbsp;&nbsp; own original content, and it would need to be&nbsp; verifiably never seen on the internet before. The&nbsp;&nbsp; highest-ranking areas would be called “producer’s&nbsp; lounges” and include the worst types of content,&nbsp;&nbsp; as these people would share their own&nbsp; personal experiences making these videos,&nbsp;&nbsp; and give tips on how others could do the same.&nbsp; This tier required proof of original production,&nbsp;&nbsp; and they would usually do this by holding&nbsp; a sign with the website’s name. In order to&nbsp;&nbsp; create their content, some of the users seemed to&nbsp; take advantage of jobs that gave them access. For&nbsp;&nbsp; example, one user who went by “Jabber” worked in a&nbsp; facility for mentally-impaired children in the UK. The forum side of the site was equally&nbsp; as concerning. One investigation found&nbsp;&nbsp; a post where children were allegedly&nbsp; being rented for the price of $4,000,&nbsp;&nbsp; or alternatively, sold into sl•very for&nbsp; $10,000. The user confirmed that he had&nbsp;&nbsp; personally spent around $30,000, but had to&nbsp; pay extra as a penalty… because they d•ed. Eventually, users who did not submit&nbsp; original content would be labeled&nbsp;&nbsp; as “leeches” and and basically banned&nbsp; from hurt2thecore, to the point where&nbsp;&nbsp; they could only access the front page. This&nbsp; made it have a very exclusive feel and led to&nbsp;&nbsp; many users getting competitive with creating&nbsp; their own content, and there were literally&nbsp;&nbsp; ranks and leaderboards to gamify everything&nbsp; and give recognition to the most active users.&nbsp;&nbsp; This system also had the very relevant side&nbsp; effect of making the site impenetrable for&nbsp;&nbsp; law enforcement. When an agent tried to pose&nbsp; as a user to gain information on the site,&nbsp;&nbsp; they would simply get kicked out and labeled as&nbsp; a leech, since obviously the agent would not be&nbsp;&nbsp; contributing their own videos. They would never be&nbsp; able to access the higher ranks of the community&nbsp;&nbsp; that were locked behind contributions,&nbsp; and thus, Lux continued to evade capture. Chapter: The King of Hurtcore By this point, Lux was on&nbsp;&nbsp; cloud nine. He apparently had delusions of&nbsp; “omnipotence and grandiosity". Not only was&nbsp;&nbsp; he a renowned security expert in the community,&nbsp; but he now owned the largest empire of hurtcore&nbsp;&nbsp; content on the dark web. His sites had a&nbsp; global reach with 400,000 hits per day,&nbsp;&nbsp; hurt2thecore alone was getting 15,000 daily&nbsp; video downloaders. He was the superadmin of&nbsp;&nbsp; all these massive communities, and bragged&nbsp; about his accolades, saying he had quote&nbsp;&nbsp; “complete control over the large group of proven&nbsp; producers on any site”. He also stroked his ego by&nbsp;&nbsp; strictly moderating all of these communities&nbsp; himself, banning anyone who broke the rules&nbsp;&nbsp; or uploaded bootleg content. Even among the&nbsp; others users of the dark web, he was feared,&nbsp;&nbsp; and his influence was respected. Universally,&nbsp; everyone considered him “the king of hurtcore". Because he had now become one of the&nbsp; most famous people on the dark web,&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux started to make connections with other notable&nbsp; individuals from similar communities. Among them,&nbsp;&nbsp; he became close with Peter Scully, who remains&nbsp; perhaps the most infamous and despised content&nbsp;&nbsp; producer of all time. Lux reached out to him&nbsp; because he was interested in obtaining one of his&nbsp;&nbsp; creations, which was known as one of the darkest&nbsp; and most brutal hurtcore videos ever created.&nbsp;&nbsp; Known as “Daisy’s Destruction or “DD””, this&nbsp; film had only been seen by a select few,&nbsp;&nbsp; but Lux made it his ultimate goal to obtain a&nbsp; copy of it, and then host it on Hurt2thecore. After searching for a while, he was&nbsp; connected with a production company&nbsp;&nbsp; called “no limits fun”, who claimed to have&nbsp; Daisy’s Destruction in their possession.&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux started by offering them $900 in bitcoin—an&nbsp; offer that was immediately rejected. Apparently,&nbsp;&nbsp; the going rate was at least $10,000 dollars to&nbsp; view the film. The business relationship between&nbsp;&nbsp; these two parties quickly deteriorated after&nbsp; that discussion. Lux did not give up though, and&nbsp;&nbsp; a while later, was still able to obtain a few&nbsp; short sections of the film from another unknown&nbsp;&nbsp; source. This was all he needed — he knew&nbsp; that if he released the footage for free,&nbsp;&nbsp; he would simultaneously get revenge on “no&nbsp; limits fun”, and become even more powerful. Chapter: Infamy As he slowly releasing the footage,&nbsp;&nbsp; it turned out to be just as horrifying as&nbsp; the rumors had suggested. The subject of&nbsp;&nbsp; the video… was 18 months old. It was called&nbsp; quote “one of the worst things you could&nbsp;&nbsp; see”. Lux had just delivered the most infamous&nbsp; hurtcore video in the history of the internet,&nbsp;&nbsp; and he had done what everyone thought&nbsp; was impossible. This skyrocketed him&nbsp;&nbsp; to stardom in the community, but also,&nbsp; other communities began to hate him. Even among MAPs on the dark web, many of them&nbsp; drew the line at hurtcore. They were totally fine&nbsp;&nbsp; with the so-called ordinary forms of content that&nbsp; they saw on other websites, but the extreme level&nbsp;&nbsp; of stuff that Lux was hosting began to be seen as&nbsp; unethical, even among THESE types of communities.&nbsp;&nbsp; The release of Daisy’s Destruction film was the&nbsp; tipping point for some of them as well, making&nbsp;&nbsp; the name “LUX” synonymous with… cruelty. His&nbsp; reputation became tied to hosting, encouraging,&nbsp;&nbsp; and enabling the t•rture of hundreds, and&nbsp; potentially… thousands. Going forward,&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux began to receive about a dozen d•ath&nbsp; threats every week. Obviously though, none&nbsp;&nbsp; of those people could follow through with their&nbsp; threats, as they had no idea WHO Lux really was. In order to address some of&nbsp; the criticism he was receiving,&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux opened up a new site called “love2thecore”,&nbsp; which was a sister site of hurt2thecore. This&nbsp;&nbsp; site basically had the exact same structure,&nbsp; but it didn’t allow the h•rm-related content&nbsp;&nbsp; that offended many people in the community.&nbsp; He called it the “softer side” of his empire,&nbsp;&nbsp; but even then, Lux’s reputation kept some&nbsp; people away. One member said “It ain’t worth&nbsp;&nbsp; being associated with someone personally&nbsp; responsible for the distribution of DD.”&nbsp;&nbsp; It seemed at this point that Lux REALLY&nbsp; was the most HATED man on the dark web. Especially following the escalation of his crimes,&nbsp; the FBI and other law enforcement were spending&nbsp;&nbsp; more time than ever hunting for Lux. They managed&nbsp; to identify and arrest several of his associates&nbsp;&nbsp; in the community, but unfortunately, those&nbsp; people ALSO had no way to identify him. The&nbsp;&nbsp; FBI investigations continued to come up empty,&nbsp; until they finally caught a break in 2014. They&nbsp;&nbsp; received an anonymous report that had a lead, and&nbsp; it suggested Lux might be located in Melbourne,&nbsp;&nbsp; Australia. This obviously did not line up with&nbsp; his whole story about being a doctor from the US,&nbsp;&nbsp; but the timezone DID align with the hours&nbsp; that he was active on the websites. This&nbsp;&nbsp; made the FBI hopeful, and they launched a joint&nbsp; investigation involving interpol and Victorian&nbsp;&nbsp; police to further investigate the matter. It&nbsp; was now a full-scale international manhunt,&nbsp;&nbsp; all stemming from that simple tip that&nbsp; probably came from one of Lux’s haters. Chapter: Law Enforcement Closes In&nbsp; Lux certainly knew that he was a prime target&nbsp; for the FBI, and he had continued to taunt them.&nbsp;&nbsp; At this point though, he somehow felt that law&nbsp; enforcement was closing in on him. He saw many&nbsp;&nbsp; of his close friends get arrested, and maybe, he&nbsp; thought this was a sign that he would be next.&nbsp;&nbsp; It’s unclear exactly what caused him to feel this&nbsp; way, but it became very clear that he was spooked,&nbsp;&nbsp; and concerned that he might soon be&nbsp; arrested. Fearing that the end was near,&nbsp;&nbsp; he sent out a desperate email to the FBI’s&nbsp; cybercrimes tip line: “‘My name is Lux and&nbsp;&nbsp; not only do I run the largest online suite of C•&nbsp; websites on Tor, but I also have a knowledge about&nbsp;&nbsp; its users (and their identities) unrivalled by&nbsp; anyone out there.” I am sure that access to this,&nbsp;&nbsp; let alone everything else I am offering you, is&nbsp; merit for the conditions I will outline below.” After that, he demanded $50,000 in bitcoin,&nbsp; and complete immunity in all jurisdictions,&nbsp;&nbsp; in exchange for control of his PedoEmpire. The&nbsp; ability to control the Lux online persona and&nbsp;&nbsp; identify thousands of users would definitely&nbsp; be helpful, but the FBI wanted Lux himself,&nbsp;&nbsp; and they were not willing to give him immunity and&nbsp; a bunch of money. Suffice it to say, they had no&nbsp;&nbsp; interest in accepting this bizarre offer, and they&nbsp; were still following up on that anonymous tip. Lux was evidently angry about this result,&nbsp; replying to the email with “It is clear you&nbsp;&nbsp; are not taking my offer seriously”, along&nbsp; with other pleas for the FBI to reconsider.&nbsp;&nbsp; When they did not oblige, he found himself in&nbsp; a difficult position. He obviously loved the&nbsp;&nbsp; power and attention that he was receiving from&nbsp; the dark web community, but at the same time,&nbsp;&nbsp; he was paranoid that his identity could be&nbsp; exposed if he continued on much longer. And so,&nbsp;&nbsp; with that in mind, Lux made the announcement that&nbsp; he was shutting down PedoEmpire on June 24th,&nbsp;&nbsp; 2014. He felt that this was&nbsp; necessary to avoid capture. Chapter: The End of an Era&nbsp; “‘Today is the day that I walk away. There&nbsp; are personal issues which my close friends&nbsp; have been made aware of that have forced&nbsp; me to make this decision.” The so-called&nbsp;&nbsp; “personal issue” was just code for not&nbsp; wanting to go to prison, but regardless,&nbsp;&nbsp; the Lux empire was officially…. no more.&nbsp; “All Empires eventually fall and today is&nbsp;&nbsp; the day that the PedoEmpire falls,” “I don’t&nbsp; like long awkward farewells, so… Goodbye.” Members of his communities posted content in&nbsp; his honor, with one user posting an image of his&nbsp;&nbsp; 9-year old niece holding a note that read “Lux,&nbsp; you’ll be missed”. The king of hurtcore simply&nbsp;&nbsp; vanished overnight, and retreated to posting&nbsp; on other accounts in order to avoid detection. Chapter: Investigating a Suspect Still building off of that anonymous tip,&nbsp;&nbsp; law enforcement had narrowed their list of&nbsp; suspects down to just 1. After years of looking,&nbsp;&nbsp; they were confident they had their man. An officer&nbsp; said “for sure we knew who it was”, as they closed&nbsp;&nbsp; in on a house in Melbourne, Australia. Based&nbsp; on their research, the house contained a white&nbsp;&nbsp; male in his 50s, along with his wife, son, and&nbsp; daughter. The man was a working-class mechanic,&nbsp;&nbsp; and his age and time zone aligned with the Lux&nbsp; persona, even though he wasn’t actually a doctor. As officers approached the house, nothing really&nbsp; appeared to be out of the ordinary. It was just an&nbsp;&nbsp; average looking cul-de-sac in the suburbs, and as&nbsp; they searched the house and basically turned the&nbsp;&nbsp; entire place upside down, they realized that&nbsp; they had made a serious mistake during their&nbsp;&nbsp; investigation; they learned that their suspect,&nbsp; the 50-year old man, appeared to know literally&nbsp;&nbsp; nothing about computers. Police were confused&nbsp; because just hours earlier they were feeling&nbsp;&nbsp; confident, so they started digging a little&nbsp; deeper by talking to the rest of his family.&nbsp;&nbsp; His wife and daughter were almost certainly not&nbsp; behind the Lux persona, so that is when his son&nbsp;&nbsp; became the new primary suspect. He was a 21-year&nbsp; old who spent most of his time in his room,&nbsp;&nbsp; struggled to make friends, and spent&nbsp; endless hours on world of warcraft.&nbsp;&nbsp; He also had literally no presence on any social&nbsp; media, and police considered this “very odd” given&nbsp;&nbsp; that he was terminally online, and most people his&nbsp; age were active on at least one social platform. Unfortunately for the police, they had very little&nbsp; evidence that the mechanic’s son was actually Lux.&nbsp;&nbsp; They asked him to unlock his laptop and hand&nbsp; over his other electronics, but he refused,&nbsp;&nbsp; and they didn’t have enough proof to forcibly take&nbsp; away all his equipment, or even make an arrest.&nbsp;&nbsp; They were about to turn away empty handed,&nbsp; but that is when he made a critical mistake…&nbsp;&nbsp; The 21-year old voluntarily decided to unlock&nbsp; his phone, and let police take a look at what was&nbsp;&nbsp; inside. At first, they found literally nothing&nbsp; — and this is precisely why he offered to hand&nbsp;&nbsp; it over. There was no evidence of any kind.&nbsp; However, when they inspected the data inside&nbsp;&nbsp; of the phone’s cache, they found that it had&nbsp; 3 images of C•. That content hidden inside the&nbsp;&nbsp; temporary memory was just enough evidence for&nbsp; them to arrest the son, named Matthew Graham. At this point, it seemed very likely that&nbsp; Matthew was indeed behind the Lux persona,&nbsp;&nbsp; and he was charged with possessing the&nbsp; images. However, police were not able to&nbsp;&nbsp; break through the encryption on his computer,&nbsp; and with only 3 images to use as evidence,&nbsp;&nbsp; they were not able to hold him indefinitely.&nbsp; In fact, he was soon going to be released,&nbsp;&nbsp; and this was a huge problem because&nbsp; that would give Matthew a chance to&nbsp;&nbsp; erase any additional evidence, and&nbsp; effectively wipe his slate clean. Because they were now extremely motivated to&nbsp; break into his hard drives as fast as possible,&nbsp;&nbsp; police sent one of the drives to Europol in&nbsp; Germany, and another to the FBI in Washington&nbsp;&nbsp; DC. It’s unclear exactly what happened after&nbsp; that because the court records are redacted,&nbsp;&nbsp; but eventually, the FBI was able to uncover&nbsp; irrefutable proof that Lux… was Matthew Graham.&nbsp;&nbsp; Armed with this confirmation, an investigator&nbsp; proceeded to pay him a visit in Australian jail,&nbsp;&nbsp; and Lux was given an ultimatum: either he was&nbsp; going to be charged with all of these offenses&nbsp;&nbsp; in the United States, OR, he could stay in&nbsp; Australia, but only if he openly admitted to&nbsp;&nbsp; everything. He decided to remain in Australia&nbsp; after some consideration, and he also had to&nbsp;&nbsp; hand over his password, which was 64 characters&nbsp; long. At long last, law enforcement had access&nbsp;&nbsp; to his hard drives, and as expected, huge&nbsp; amounts of C• and incriminating evidence… Chapter: The Trial Lux pleaded guilty to&nbsp;&nbsp; 13 charges in court, and during his trial,&nbsp; even more strange details came to light.&nbsp;&nbsp; Lux described himself as a quote “strong&nbsp; believer in free speech”, and according to him,&nbsp;&nbsp; this justified all of his behavior. He even denied&nbsp; that he had any interest in children at all,&nbsp;&nbsp; instead describing himself as as•xual, even&nbsp; though that seemed incredibly unlikely. It&nbsp;&nbsp; seems even more unlikely when you take into&nbsp; account that he previously admitted he was&nbsp;&nbsp; attracted to 10 year-olds, when speaking to a&nbsp; US journalist. His parents were shocked when&nbsp;&nbsp; they learned Matthew had built this entire&nbsp; empire under their roof, as they were under&nbsp;&nbsp; the impression he was just gaming all day. They&nbsp; sobbed in disbelief during his trial, and later,&nbsp;&nbsp; they found graffiti in his closet reading “Parents&nbsp; should be afraid of raising children like us”. One of Lux’s most notable charges was aiding and&nbsp; abetting crimes committed in Russia. He was in&nbsp;&nbsp; close contact with an a•user who proceeded&nbsp; to carry out an a•duction, and ultimately,&nbsp;&nbsp; a m•rder. Lux advised him exactly what to do,&nbsp; how to record it, and even promised a monetary&nbsp;&nbsp; incentive. “I have many contacts willing to&nbsp; purchase such a video”, he said. Similarly,&nbsp;&nbsp; another charge referenced Lux encouraging&nbsp; the mistreatment of a 7-year old from the UK,&nbsp;&nbsp; who was disabled, mute, and in a wheelchair. He&nbsp; added, "if you record audio then that can give a&nbsp;&nbsp; lot away but it's good the fact she can't speak,&nbsp; I don't think it's going to narrow it down". Another unsettling detail that came to light is&nbsp; that Lux was actively interacting with children&nbsp;&nbsp; in person, quite often. He was working as a&nbsp; babysitter for 6 different kids while he was&nbsp;&nbsp; actively running the empire, obviously to the&nbsp; surprise of everyone else involved. There is&nbsp;&nbsp; no evidence that he actually did anything to&nbsp; the children he was watching over, but given&nbsp;&nbsp; his other charges, it’s crazy to think that he was&nbsp; frequently left alone with kids for hours on end. As a part of the trial, the prosecutor insisted&nbsp; that the judge view Daisy’s Destruction video in&nbsp;&nbsp; order to properly understand what this case was&nbsp; really about. The judge kept putting it off,&nbsp;&nbsp; but eventually, he went into his chambers&nbsp; to watch the material. When he returned….&nbsp;&nbsp; He was visibly pale and disturbed. He said&nbsp; it was “the worst thing <he> had ever seen”,&nbsp;&nbsp; adding that Matthew’s case was quote&nbsp; “without parallel.” Several people had to&nbsp;&nbsp; remove themselves from the courtroom because the&nbsp; description of these videos was so traumatizing. It’s impossible to describe all&nbsp; of his charges in a YouTube video,&nbsp;&nbsp; but the trial explored each&nbsp; crime in excruciating detail,&nbsp;&nbsp; and Matthew admitted to everything. At&nbsp; the end, the judge summarized the crimes,&nbsp;&nbsp; calling them “pure evil”, and stating that this&nbsp; was quote “the worst thing [he] had ever seen". Chapter: Sentencing&nbsp; On March 17th, 2016, Matthew David Graham&nbsp; was found guilty on all 13 charges,&nbsp;&nbsp; and sentenced to 65 years in prison….&nbsp; Is what I was expecting to say during&nbsp;&nbsp; this part of the video. In reality, Lux&nbsp; was given a sentence of just… 15 years.&nbsp;&nbsp; I truly fail to see how this is possible after&nbsp; everyone called his offenses quote “the worst&nbsp;&nbsp; crimes imaginable”, but he was also given the&nbsp; possibility of parole after 10 years. Since&nbsp;&nbsp; he had already served 570 days at the time of&nbsp; sentencing, this means Lux is already eligible for&nbsp;&nbsp; parole right now, at the time of recording this&nbsp; video. Any day now he could become a free man,&nbsp;&nbsp; and he is guaranteed to be reintegrated with&nbsp; society by 2029 when his sentence expires. When news of this spread to the public,&nbsp; people from all over the world criticized&nbsp;&nbsp; the short sentence. On the dark web, some&nbsp; of his biggest haters were outraged as well,&nbsp;&nbsp; stating that they “despised” him. On the&nbsp; other hand though, some celebrated the result,&nbsp;&nbsp; saying stuff like quote “Cannot wait to see&nbsp; him in 15 years. He’ll still be quite young&nbsp;&nbsp; to admin a site, really.”. Others commented&nbsp; about how his sentence might’ve differed if&nbsp;&nbsp; he chose to be extradited to the US. As he is&nbsp; one of the most prolific offenders of all time,&nbsp;&nbsp; it seems that this could’ve&nbsp; resulted in a longer sentence. There is not any public information&nbsp; about Matthew’s status as of today,&nbsp;&nbsp; and there is no way to tell if he will resume his&nbsp; activities on the dark web when he is released.&nbsp;&nbsp; One YouTube video claimed Lux was seen using&nbsp; prison computers to search for “petite women”,&nbsp;&nbsp; suggesting that he has not reformed even the&nbsp; slightest amount, but I was not able to verify&nbsp;&nbsp; that this claim is true. There were also reports&nbsp; that Lux is being bullied and assaulted in prison,&nbsp;&nbsp; by both the inmates, and even the prison guards.&nbsp; Once they learned of Lux’s crimes and realized&nbsp;&nbsp; they were dealing with the most hated man&nbsp; on the dark web, they didn’t hold back. Perhaps the only silver lining is that&nbsp; multiple pr•dators were arrested based&nbsp;&nbsp; on the information that was extracted from&nbsp; Lux’s empire. With him acting as an informant,&nbsp;&nbsp; agencies were able to bring down other&nbsp; international offenders, leading to the&nbsp;&nbsp; rescue of 30 young victims. Australia also made a&nbsp; new law because of him, making it now a criminal&nbsp;&nbsp; offense to withhold the password to an encrypted&nbsp; device when given a lawful order to do so. Ultimately, it’s concerning that Lux&nbsp; will soon be re-entering society,&nbsp;&nbsp; and IT sets a dangerous&nbsp; precedent for other offenders.&nbsp;&nbsp; IT sends the message that crimes like his aren’t&nbsp; taken very seriously. If the KING of the empire&nbsp;&nbsp; is allowed to parole after just 10 years, how&nbsp; lenient is the punishment for his subjects?

and if you do too click that subscribe button and hit that bell so you don't miss any videos hunters and hikers aren't enemies we're just using the same public lands to enjoy the outdoors and we need to respect each other and in my opinion hunters deserve our respect there is no other single group in the united states that has done more for the conservation of public lands and access to public lands than hunters so we need to find a way to mutually use these lands and at the end of the day we're both just looking to get outside and enjoy ourselves tip number one is education you need to understand when hunting season is typically hunting season runs from october to december but even within the hunting season there's different levels for example right now it's actually both season in my area for deer and you can use a gun to shoot small game like squirrels this weekend opens up rifle and shotgun for deer so understanding where the hunters are going to be located what they're going to be hunting for and what method they're going to be hunting for will help us understand where we should be hiking this weekend we move from bow hunting to shotgun and rifle and the level of danger between those two is different during bow hunting season it's not as dangerous because the hunter needs to be within a very close proximity of the deer to shoot a deer with a bow however when it's rifle and shotgun they can hit a deer from 300 yards away and that bullet can ricochet in a bunch of different areas so the level of risk is different depending on what game is open and what the hunters can use to shoot that game next part of education is knowing where hunting is actually allowed in your parks a lot of town parks and county parks hunting is usually prohibited however the state has several different lands and designations where hunting is acceptable in state forests and multiple use areas hunting is typically open season in some state parks hunting is allowed by permit only believe it or not in letchworth state park you can hunt with the permit part of the education is understanding the boundaries of those state lands and where you're going to go into private or public land you'll usually see a posted sign which means you're moving into private land and you want to stay away from there because the landowner has every right to hunt on his own land tip number two is wear bright clothing you can pick up a vest like this you can have a bright colored bandana either red or orange or yellow you can also use other bright colored clothing for instance this jacket you just want to really stand out against the canopy of the forest camouflage probably not a good idea also plan ahead for hunting season i buy all of my gear in bright colors my camo black is in orange my osprey is another orange and this serves two purposes one it gives you some bright colors for hunting season plus if you fall down a ravine or something your friends can see you a little bit better so when you're talking about bright clothing don't forget about your dogs i use a red bandana or bright orange bright yellow or they make vests for your dogs something that's going to make them stand out i've never actually seen a deer wear a bandana so i'm hoping that a hunter won't mistake my dog for a deer and here's my other dog okay and since we're on the topic of the dogs tip number three is keep your dogs on a leash it's usually the rule in most state lands to have your dog on a leash i know a lot of people don't but during hunting season it is so important for you to keep your dog on a leash that hunter is not going to mistake your dog if you're walking right behind it with a leash on so please for the sake of your dog please keep them on a leash during hunting season tip number four is stay on designated trails and make some noise we smell and we make a lot of noise hunters want to get as far away from hikers as possible so when you're on the trail make sure you stay on a designated trail and also if you're with a friend strike up a conversation so a hunter knows you're there and finally number five is avoid hiking in dusk and dawn typically dusk and dawn is the prime time for hunters to be out hunting if you avoid those periods you're going to avoid the hunters and everybody's going to be happy as i said before hikers and hunters need to respect each other we're both out there just to have a good time and enjoy the land so if you follow these simple rules you'll avoid confrontations with hunters and everybody's going to have a good time so i step in becoming a hunter this course is designed and administered state by state again it's administered underneath your state's Fish and Game Department so it's not a one size fits all you'll need to take it again so even if you are very early in your journey to become a hunter you can take your hunter safety course now and then that will be good throughout the rest of your lifetime so what can you expect out of a hunter safety course for the most part they are designed to teach you basic firearm safety general hunter safety and they don't do a great job of really teaching you how to hunt but they're more focused on keeping you safe in the woods especially when it comes to firearms now if you plan on archery hunting although it's not a requirement in every state there is something called bow hunters education and again some states do require it some states don't so please I highly highly encourage you to again find your state's Department down below and then read that website   10. @lamentofmidna [2 likes]: Thank you for talking about this. It takes a lot to admit your past mistakes, but its a very important topic for this community. More people need to realize that toxicity is poisoning the game, because it takes away your ability to reflect on your own gameplay. You can’t get better if you can’t see your mistakes bc you’re too busy blaming everyone else. 

I used to get really angry over idv. Randoms throwing my matches or a win turned to a loss bc people waited at the gate, failed rescues, 5 second kites, chaired in basement, etc. I used to fight about it in post match, not telling people to off themselves, but “clearly” they messed up and can’t understand that and I needed to let them know. But luckily I started watching a streamer/youtuber who said its important to look at the match as a whole, and have empathy for your teammates. Yeah maybe they threw the match, but what could you have done better as well? What can you learn from this match? And maybe they’re having an off day, maybe their game crashed or a parent called them away. Maybe they just made a mistake or they got outplayed by the Hunter. Sometimes the Hunter is just better, and that’s okay. It sucks in the moment, but at the end of the day you move on to the next match. You learn and you get better.

That made me realize my anger wasn’t helping me or anyone else. Its okay to be upset, but aiming it at others isn’t going to solve anything. Now I’d like to give constructive criticism to help my teammates in a positive way, so they can get better. But people only see it as an attack and get defensive or want to fight. 

I’ve started being positive in postmatch chat instead. If someone does a good kite, rescue, etc I let them know. It usually makes them happy and feel appreciated. It helps my mood a lot, having positive interactions instead of negative. And makes the game feel a little less toxic for a moment.

  11. @LoveNotWar-c6y: I wouldent wear cotton, only wool. The cotton gets damp, stays damp forever, you lose all potential insulation value from wet cotton, will develope foot blisters and thighs/ass chaffing, and withing 20 minutes you will begin to smell like a wet fart to every deer within 100 yards of you. I would wear one layer of marino wool on your skin and stack layers of regular wool on top of that and cover that with a thin Gortex outer layer. If you get hot/cold remove/add layers. Also remove/cover anything glossy such as your skin, eyes, teeth, watchs, buckles, even certain clothes have a kind of plastic reflective sheen etc etc. Dont use any black/white colors because they dont exist in nature. Also deer are hardwired to "see" green and browns and that camo is useless and you would be better served with simply spray painting plain clothes with random streaks of earth colors like greys and tan colors. Anything that will break up your bodys profile. If you cover your face with a veil, sit under a tree, and stay still, the majority of people will never see you. Atleast, until you move and break the optical illusion and then its over and its time to explain why you were in a tree stand at the Park.
  12. @nickoneill4323: Walmart was the only place that sold the brush camo pattern that was perfect for my area, unfortunately it’s discontinued now but man Walmart has never let me down for hunting

- Busting the Myth of "Men Hunt, Women Gather": Kind: captions Language: en earlier this week biotech company pfizer announced that the vaccine it's developing for covet 19 is 90 effective and that is very exciting news it also comes with some pretty big caveats including the fact that they haven't formally published peer-reviewed results yet we're going to spend some time working on that story we don't want to come to you with something rushed we want to think it through talk it through figure it out all the little details here and there so that's coming next week until then enjoy this news from the world of paleoanthropology there's a persistent stereotype that our early human ancestors had clear gender roles men were hunters while women were gatherers and foragers while that's obviously an overly simplistic picture of diverse and complex social structures research published last week in science advances presented evidence that some of the earliest big game hunters were female back in 2013 a local indigenous man found a 9 000 year old burial site about 4 000 meters high in the andes mountains in peru then in 2018 a research group visited the site with permission from the local community where they found multiple sets of human remains hunting tools including projectile points and other tools for hunting and processing animals since folks were often buried with their earthly possessions it was fair to assume that one of the people in the graves was a hunter during their lifetime the team's bone expert determined that the set of bones was probably female by the looks of their femur and back in the lab an analysis of proteins from the subject's teeth confirmed that it was a female skeleton this analysis involved amylogenin a protein in teeth that can come from either the x or y chromosome and looks slightly different in each case now a single burial doesn't necessarily mean scientists throw out everything they know about prehistoric division of labor so the researchers started looking for patterns in other early human burials they looked through documentation of burials in the americas from around the same time they narrowed their results to just skeletons that were buried with similar big game hunting tools and came up with 27 individuals 16 of them were male while 11 including the new skeleton were female according to the researchers this is evidence that women not only participated in the hunting activities but might have accounted for up to half of the hunting population their society's diet may have consisted mostly of animal meat so it would make sense to have all hands on deck to help hunt that conclusion comes with some caveats though we can say very little about how these ancient people felt about gender and gender roles since this study mostly only tells us about their physiology but we can say that the cultural position of these individuals doesn't match up with what we've assumed the researchers limited their search to the americas so we can't necessarily say that early humans in asia or africa had similar social setups plus many present-day cultures including hunter-gatherers do seem to fall into this gender divide where men do most of the hunting since that's the case though this study raises some new questions it tells us we can't project modern understandings of gender roles onto the past but that means we'd like to know how gender divisions of labor changed over time and from place to place another recently published article in nature ecology and evolution also gives us a better understanding of gender and human bodies albeit from much much earlier before this study we thought that males from the extinct human species paranthropus robustus were much larger than females a phenomenon called sexual dimorphism that's because when previous research looked at fossilized parenthropus remains found in south africa it seemed like the males were noticeably bigger than the females these guys lived alongside our ancestors members of the genus homo about 2 million years ago this new research describes a paranthropus specimen that's about 200 000 years older but here's the thing a newly found nearly complete skull was identified as male and was much smaller than expected that made the researchers think that the size difference wasn't sexual dimorphism but just differences between the two samples of fossils with that in mind the researchers had a new question to tackle if parenthropus used to be smaller how and why did they get bigger over time well around the time parenthropus and homo showed up another early human relative australopithecus was on its way out and the climate in the region got much cooler and drier which led to rapid evolutionary change as these early humans adjusted to eating much tougher plants the idea is that parathropus and homo adjusted way better than australopithecus did and part of that adjustment may have had to do with how paranthropus chewed their food the newly found much older specimen had a less powerful jawn chewing muscles which could mean that they evolved a stronger bite force and bigger teeth to process those tough plants better and that made the researchers think the overall size difference could have been another one of these evolutionary adjustments the researchers used these findings to caution others in their field about making assumptions based on a limited fossil record knowing what kind of evolutionary pressure early humans faced gives us a more complete picture of how modern bodies came to be a thing and of course it reminds us to keep our assumptions about sex and gender in check we feel a little bit safer in our assumptions of what science fans like and if you're looking for the perfect gift for such folks - 6 Myths About Hunters | Debunking Some Common Misconceptions For New Hunters: Kind: captions Language: en Hey guys, thanks for tuning in. It is a beautiful spring day here in Colorado, even though we didn't actually have a winter, but that's neither here nor there. But it's time to start thinking about getting outside. And if you're new to hunting or thinking about getting into hunting, you might have some ideas in your head about who hunters are. So, I thought I'd just take a few minutes and debunk some myths about the club that you'll be joining should you decide to start hunting. So, let's get right into it. Myth number one, we're all conservative. That is Now, a study that was done in 2012 found that about 50% of hunters identified as conservative, about 37 identified as in the middle or moderate, and about 10% identified as liberal. That seems to track. Now, I will say unless you've been living under a rock, um, much has changed in the last 14 years when it comes to politics and people who identified as either liberal liberal or conservative u might have changed their viewpoints a little bit since, you know, 2016. Um, but it gives you a good general breakdown of the types of people who'll be out there. And though yes, 50% of hunters, assuming that study is accurate, are conservative, that means 50% are not. I can tell you that the people who taught me to hunt, and most of the guys I hunt with um are either liberal or in the middle for the most part. And I'd say every other person I meet out in the woods is pretty conservative. Um so tracks, I'd say that's a pretty accurate thing. But you don't have to be right-wing to hunt, even though a lot of the hunting content out there would have you think so. We wear orange hats, not red. Myth number two. We don't love animals. Nothing could be further from the truth. Honestly, I am a huge animal lover. Everybody I hunt with is a huge God damn. Stop. Stop. Myth number two, we don't love animals. I understand why people might think that if you've never been exposed to the hunting world, um, and and you see people taking an animal's life, uh, I get how your brain goes there. I really do. But it really isn't true. In fact, I'm a huge animal lover. I used to want to be a vet when I was a kid and I couldn't stop stomach the thought of killing animals and so I decided not to be a vet and the first time I went out hunting when I was 12 years old I was terrified of killing something. Um and to this day I still feel a little bit guilty every time I do harvest an animal. Um, most hunters I know are huge animal lovers. And in fact, even though hunting played a role in the eradication or near extinction of a lot of species throughout the world, it's also hunters who've brought them back from that. And it's our love for them that has contributed to conservation. And yes, it does require a little bit of mental compartmentalization to justify the killing while loving them. Um, and I it's hard to explain how that feels, but it's true. Um, most of us really really love the animals and study them and get to know them and spend have just as much fun watching them in the field and not pulling the trigger. And to me, that's very important. Myth number three, we're just in it for the trophy. Again, wrong. Now, I'm not saying that there isn't an element to that where if you have two bull elk standing in front of you and one has a massive rack and the other one's me, um, yeah, you're probably going to go for the bigger one. But for most of us, that's not why we get into it. And in fact, you are required to harvest at least a good chunk of the meat off of big game animals in just about every state in the country. Now, there are exceptions for things like coyotes and uh wolves, some of the furbears, and if you're trapping and that kind of stuff, and that's a whole other conversation, but if you hunt deer, elk, analopee, bear, turkey, most birds, um you have to harvest it legally. But even if it wasn't required by law, everybody I hunt with eats what they kill. And if you get more than you need or more than your family wants, you give it to other people or you can donate it to homeless shelters. Just about every state has some sort of donation system set up so that if you have a surplus of meat, you can donate it to either women's shelters or homeless shelters or indigenous communities. There's never a reason not to harvest the meat. But it is not only required by law. It is the practice of most hunters to harvest what they kill and not just do it for the trophy. Myth number four, we're all dudes. Again, not true. A 2022 survey by the US Fish and Wildlife Service found that out of about 14.4 million hunters nationwide, 19% were women. So, when you go out in the woods, about one in five of the hunters you're going to run into are going to be female. I would say that tracks with what I've seen out there. And honestly, there's no reason not to have more women out in the field. There's nothing that a man can do better when it comes to shooting or tracking or any of the hunting activities that men are better at. Sure. Like maybe if hunting involved wrestling elk and stabbing them with a knife, maybe we'd have a leg up. Although we'd probably probably still get killed. Um but otherwise, women are just as good at it. Now, you're going to run into a lot of dicks when you're out there in the hunting world, but it's not a requirement to have one. Myth number five, it's all white guys. I will say it is still majority white. About 80% identify as white. About 9% identify as black, about 6% are Asian-American, and about 10% identify as other, which could be Pacific Islander, Native American, uh, people, multiple races. And as far as ethnicity goes, 11% identified as Hispanic. And I would say that is pretty true. And it really depends on where you are in the country. When I go hunting back in Virginia and West Virginia and even a good chunk of Colorado, yeah, it's mostly white people. But when I hunted in California, it was mostly Hispanic people I'd see out there. And when I go down to Southern Colorado in certain areas, there's a lot of Native Americans who make up the majority of the people you're going to run into out there. So, there's a bit of everybody out there. Even though it's still mostly white, there's still hundreds and hundreds of thousands of hunters who of different races, millions of different races. And it really depends on the local demographic as to who you're going to see when you go out there. Myth number six, we're all straight. I don't have any numbers to actually back up this claim, but I can tell you I do know people who are LGBTQ plus who are in the hunting world. And I would imagine that because even though conservatives are only half of the demographic, um that isn't always the most welcoming community. And I think that uh there's a lot of hunters who probably aren't open about their sexuality, which is a shame. Um, but I can tell you, we've all seen Brokeback Mountain. We all know there's gay cowboys and gay rodeo stars. There's no reason why there aren't a bunch of gay hunters, too. And maybe not everybody goes up there to hunt. Folks, I want to remind you that just like so many things in life, the loudest voices rarely make up the majority. And even though when you watch hunting content, you're going to see a lot of straight white conservative men talking about guns and long beards and shaved heads. That isn't who we all are. And if you're hesitant to get started or nervous about it because you think that maybe you don't look like the typical hunter and you don't think like the typical hunter, you're not the right sex or sexual orientation and you won't find a home in the hunting community. I'm here to tell you that you will. We want you to join. We need you to join. It's the diversity of this country that makes us great. in a diversity of the hunting community that's going to protect not only the animals but our lands and our rights going forward. So please do not feel unwelcome. You are welcome. We want you to start hunting and you should start this year. So please get out there, be safe, have fun. Good luck.

- The ‘alpha male’ myth, debunked | Frans de Waal: Kind: captions Language: en - All social animals have social hierarchies. If you put six puppies together, they will fight over who's the highest ranking. If you put six goslings together, if you put kids together in a daycare center, they will do the same thing. All young animals, they will try to establish their rank order. But usually the first position is the most important one and that's the one that they compete over. People sometimes ask me, what does it take to be an alpha male? And they think the answer from a primatologist would be, what it takes is to be the strongest and the meanest and the most intimidating. But that's not really what an alpha male usually is. An alpha male is usually also admired. They protect the underdog, they break up fights, they have a high level of empathy. So, yeah, you may want to be an alpha male but if you're surrounding people don't see you as that, it's not going to happen. I'm Frans de Waal. I'm a primatologist and a biologist and I'm the author of the book "Different: Gender Through the Eyes of a Primatologist". I've been working with primates I think about 50 years. Mostly with chimpanzees, with bonobos but most of my work is on social behavior. The term alpha male came originally from wolf research and I just referred, I think, to the highest ranking male wolf. But to apply the term as people do today, people, usually with an alpha male, they mean a bully. Someone who beats you over the head and lets you know every day that he's the boss. I'm partly responsible for that because I wrote this book Chimpanzee Politics and used the term alpha male quite frequently. And then the Republicans in Washington, they picked up on the book. It reached also the business community who started producing books of how to become the alpha male of the company and let everyone know that you're boss and stuff like that. I feel very conflicted because the term alpha male for me is not a negative term. People always think the alpha male is a sort of personality. No, the alpha male is just a top male and he can be a very nice guy or he can be a very nasty guy. That is actually irrelevant, if he's at the top, he's the alpha male. And I think people overestimate male dominance mainly because they think purely in physical terms but primate societies are political systems and physical power is only one part of the whole equation. People don't realize that the alpha male chimpanzees, for example, you have to have supporters, you cannot do that on your own. It means that a high-ranking female can have an enormous amount of power because if she can rally all the other females behind a certain male, she has all the power there. And then, in addition, you have bonobos who are very close to us too, equally close as the chimpanzee where the alpha individual is a female. And most of these differences between the sexes are actually smaller than we think. We sort of, in our mind, we blow them up, but they're actually if you measure them, they're not so great. Robert Martin is an anthropologist who said that the difference between the sexes is a bimodal difference. What he means there is that the differences are statistical and that there's a lot of exceptions to them. For example, Donna is a chimpanzee that I've known since she was three, so very little. And already at that age, she was different from the rest. She was a female clearly, but she liked to wrestle. And when she grew older, when she became adolescent, she grew big shoulders, a lot of big hair, a big head. She started to look like a male. And she associated with the males, she hung out with them. So, that's one individual. And I've known quite a few individuals also among the males who don't exactly act like males or females who don't act like females. And so, individual variability is basically the material of evolution and that diversity I think that's the same sort of diversity that we see in human society. Sex is biological sex defined by genitals, hormones, chromosomes. Gender is much more flexible, gender is more masculine, feminine and everything in between and is, of course, very much a cultural construct in the sense that social norms make how you have to behave as a male or how you have to behave as a female and those are the gender definitions and they change with time, they change with society. So, we have all that variability. And in the other primates, I've never seen that that's a problem. So, that's something where we could learn something from them is that they take an individual like Donna as she is, they're not making a big fuss about it because they're much less normative than we are and much less ideological, of course, than we are. And so, I've never seen that these individuals who deviate a little bit from the common patterns that they get into trouble over that. Many people consider empathy as the core of human morality. You cannot have an interest in other people and wanting to help them if you don't have empathy for them. And so, empathy is the glue of our society and our moral systems would be basically impossible, I think, if we had no empathy. I'm not saying that chimps and bonobos are moral beings the way we are, but they clearly have that capacity of being interested in the state of somebody else. I think a leader needs to pay attention to the group dynamics, make sure that everyone has a voice in the group and that you, like alpha male chimpanzees, that you protect the underdog against potential violence or potential abuse. I think humans are psychologically exactly like the apes, so socially and emotionally we are like apes. We are intellectually more developed. We have language, of course, we are technologically more developed. But many of the basics of our social relationships, both in terms of the hierarchies that we have, the friendships, the attachments, in all of these ways, we are very close to the primates. We're very similar to them. And so, certainly in that area, the apes can tell us something about us because there's basically no distinction. - Get smarter faster with videos from the world's biggest thinkers. To learn even more from the world's biggest thinkers, get Big Think+ for your business.

- Why You Want a Hunting Gear Ratio in Your Vehicle: Kind: captions Language: en Have you ever wondered how final drive ratios&nbsp;got their weird numbers? Three forty-two,&nbsp;three seventy-three, four ten, four eleven,&nbsp;and four fifty-six? Why not an easy number like&nbsp;three and a half or four? Well, it comes down&nbsp;to optimal gear design, called hunting gears. With hunting gears, any given gear tooth on the&nbsp;drive gear will come in contact with every single tooth on the driven gear before reaching the&nbsp;starting point again. This has several benefits,&nbsp;such as reduced noise, better break-in, and an&nbsp;even distribution of wear. If gear teeth were to&nbsp;contact the same point more frequently, it would&nbsp;create a pattern that is more likely to generate a tone, so avoiding that as much as possible reduces&nbsp; noise. Spreading the contact around as much as&nbsp;&nbsp; possible keeps any one tooth pair from wearing an&nbsp; unusual pattern into each other, making failure&nbsp;less likely or at least pushed to a later date. So how does an engineer come up with a new hunting&nbsp;gear tooth count? Well, the math is pretty&nbsp;straightforward: make sure the drive gear and the&nbsp;&nbsp; driven gear tooth counts have no common factors.&nbsp; For example, a gear with ten teeth driving a gear&nbsp;&nbsp; with fourty teeth share the factors of two, five,&nbsp; and ten. Not a good gear pairing there. However, a&nbsp;&nbsp; gear with ten teeth driving a gear with fourty-one&nbsp; teeth share no factors, so the math checks out for&nbsp;&nbsp; these. This gets us the four ten gear ratio&nbsp; I mentioned at the beginning of the video! &nbsp; Hunting gears can be found in other places&nbsp; in vehicles, too! Transmission gears often&nbsp;&nbsp; implement a hunting gear setup for the same&nbsp; reasons we've already talked about. Cams,&nbsp;&nbsp; however, those are forced to run at a two to one&nbsp; ratio in order for the engine to run properly,&nbsp;&nbsp; so they can't be hunting gears, right? Because&nbsp; most cams are driven by belts and chains,&nbsp;&nbsp; it's actually possible for a hunting pattern to&nbsp; be implemented! The chain needs to have a number&nbsp;&nbsp; of links that aren't divisible by the number&nbsp; of teeth on either gear, and same goes for the&nbsp;&nbsp; teeth on a timing belt. The length of the belt&nbsp; or chain doesn't affect the gear ratio at all,&nbsp;&nbsp; so engineers just have to make sure the belt&nbsp; or chain has the right number of teeth for the&nbsp;&nbsp; gears and the distance it needs to travel from&nbsp; one gear to the other, and then a tensioner&nbsp;&nbsp; can handle the slack left in the system. Using prime numbers is a pretty common method&nbsp;&nbsp; for quickly finding hunting gear ratios, so&nbsp; engineering these gear systems aren't wild&nbsp;&nbsp; guessing games until a cooperative pair&nbsp;is found. Still, it's a fascinating bit&nbsp;of automotive math that keeps gears working with&nbsp;minimal issues. The only big downside to them is that someone has to come up with a hunting gear&nbsp;pair and engineer a design around them, instead of designing a component and selecting whatever&nbsp;size gears happen to fit it well with a decent&nbsp;enough ratio. For most people watching, all the hard work has been done for them, but if you're an aspiring engine designer, driveline engineer,&nbsp;or gear machinist, I wish you the best of luck.

into level one and level two categories that are certified by the C Mar level one is the bare minimum in which the maximum force that gets transferred through the armers is below 18 Kon whereas the level two is better and can absorb a higher impact with maximum transmitted force of 9 kons which can reduce the chances of an injury all three of these come with C Level Two armors on shoulder and measurements required for the jacket and you're also offered a free trial and size Exchange in case the jacket doesn't match the measurements this is what gives wera a league of its own and I investment the fkr jacket is priceless at this point why you ask because the brand has sadly discontinued making riding gears which is really unfortunate given the passion the fkr team had in making these products this comes from the desire to fuel a beginner's dream of motorcycling but as they say it's been a hell of a run but all good things do come to an end but I'm genuinely hoping that they start making riding gear again very soon because this genuinely is a great product for the price it came at knowing this doesn't exist in the market anymore I covered this jacket anyway because it deserved di menion so bottom line all three of these coming at different price points catering to different purposes do make sense in their own way with the jacket sorted now let's move on to the pants which are the last piece of riding gear that you need now with pants the safety and comfort are equally important and I believe no matter what riding pants you get apart from the safety they offer they're going to be far better than normal pants trousers or jeans with ventilation at multiple places soft materials at the bottom area and easy access to get in and out riding pants turn out to be much much more practical and sensible to be in while riding a bike and I've got two pairs of riding pants with me the rhinox advento and the V Spencer to keep you safe on impact both of these come with SE level two armors on the knees and the hips the W Spenser also gets an extra tailbone armor at the back for added protection both of these pans get abrasion resistance in the form of Cordura panels on obvious impact areas while the WIA is more generous and thoughtful while using Cordura across impact areas the rhinox uses a combination of Cordura and polyester throughout the entire surface of the panels with palm-sized pockets of mesh for ventilation above the knees the vatera has a complete mesh construction everywhere else apart from the Cordura protected areas here I like wera's approach more for being smart with protecting the obvious impact areas and focusing more on ventilation on the rest it strikes a good balance between safety and comfort and trust me that level of ventilation makes a world of a difference oh let me get oh the ventilation oh my god oh the rinox on the other hand again takes the same approach as the jacket being too serious on impact and abrasion resistance and compromising slightly on Comfort which is not a bad thing on a good day but when you're riding in traffic in the heat of Mumbai you could use some ventilation and giving a small exhaust V for the air to pass doesn't really help anyway having said that when circumstances require you to be insulated for example cold places like ladog you definitely need a pair of rhinox jackets and pants the mesh ventilation could freeze you if you plan on riding in sub 5° temperatures in terms of build quality just like the jackets both the rhinox and the WIA pants are built to last for years with the WIA having an edge with a custom fit for the pands as per your measurements for the price both of these pants are surprisingly priced similar at R 8350 for the rhinox and R 8,500 for the wera with an added cost of R 500 for the optional tailbone armor so it eventually boils down to what your purpose and utility is to choose which one fits you better as I said you first need to figure out what your purpose is and then work around on making adjustments to your riding gear to suit to different scenarios that doesn't mean that you compromise on the sole purpose of the riding gear in the first place wearing the riding gear with Zips open tucking your helmet on your elbows or not wearing the riding gear in the first place please don't do that that's if if you got the riding gear make the most use of it please with that we are at the end of our quest to safety while being on the roads with whatever gears we discussed or rather I ranted about all of these in combin ation trust my riding skills to be very honest riding gear isn't some kind of a magic pill that will make me immune to any kind of mishap on the road riding gear brands surely do want you to believe so but my riding skills and my Awareness on the road is what will eventually take me back home in one piece I surely do trust whatever little riding skills I've got and we all should but how do we trust things that we don't know things that are out of our skills reach or out of our control how do I take responsibility for the idiot on the road riding and driving alongside me how do I make sure others not screwing up things for me and causing me harm in unprecedented situations like those is where you need riding gear to not avoid impact but to reduce it I'd rather decide wearing riding gear before an accident with in good shape rather than after one with a missing patch of Flesh on my body or a head injury am I making sense if I am let me know in the comment section what do you think about   9. @BryanManning: @cliffgray, or anyone else reading this... the quick stix can open wider by changing the screws(allows prone) to the 90* position.  Allowing a lot more room between poles as well.
Also buy the quick clip part that they make to go with it, it turns it into a true bipod(and is still lighter). 
Then when hiking with them you can clip them together, but only rotate to where the poles are aligned vertically then velcro the lower legs together and you have a really strong walking stick with a  a handle(top of both poles) similar in size to the ice axe. Yet another caveat to them is..if you do a tarp/tepee setup for sleeping..this setup will also create a center pole twice(clip together in reverse, and velcro the handles together) as long  for more standing room in the tepee(I'm 6'4, and this was a game changer to figure out one night). Truly a multipurpose piece of gear! Love them.
